body_txt: Much like the Tomboy , the Ice Queen is a major character archetype — almost Always Female — which is somewhat hard to define. Her signature characteristic is that she is cold, but what exactly "cold" means can vary quite a lot. Romantic elements — or lack thereof — are often useful indicators: The Ice Queen often has a "cold" beauty, like ice, and she is similarly dangerous to touch and impossible to embrace. Those who fall in love with her usually find that she cannot - or will not - return their love. She's not much for friendship either, preferring to be alone with her thoughts . Plots where an Ice Queen "thaws" and learns to enjoy the company of others are so common that they have their own trope . Cracks in the Icy Façade may also predict future thawing. This trope is strictly about a personality type; a character having ice-related abilities is a separate trope. Still, it's common for the character who creates ice to have a frosty personality to match (though she might simply use an ice motif ). Compare the Tragic Ice Character , whose association with the cold represents her inner pain or numbness. Not to be confused with a character who has a royal title associated with ice or snow , though the two may overlap (and often do in more magical settings). An Ice Queen requires at least one "cold" personality trait to get labeled as such, and there are many different flavors. The following are some of them:  This is the supertrope for all the "frosty women" subtypes. No examples please, instead place examples in the different subtropes. If we don't have a specific subtrope, then feel free to help us create one for it .
